description: Engine + primary oil change (20W-50 HD oil)
notes: HD 2017+ service manual — 20W-50 HD oil. Forum consensus on V&H and HD forums: change annually even if under 5k miles; engines sit more than they run for weekend riders.
Sportster service manual — 0.25" free play. Forum tip: re-check 500 mi after an adjust, primary tends to seat.
HD stators are a known wear item. Inspection at 20k gives enough lead time to catch the gray-goop failure before it strands the bike.
HD service manual — Formula+ or equivalent. Forum: many riders shorten to 10k on Twin Cam for cleaner shifts.

description: Front + rear brake pad thickness check
notes: HD OEM minimum thickness 1.0 mm; replace at 2.0 mm for safety margin.

description: Drive belt + sprocket tension + wear check
notes: HD belts: 5/8" deflection w/ 10 lb load. Forum: inspect for missing teeth post-rainy-season.
Forum consensus: every 5 years or 25k, whichever first; fork oil degrades with heat cycling even without big mileage.
Touring models stretch cables fast with heavy clutch pulls; check after every major service.
Softail rear-swingarm pivot is often neglected. Mobil 1 grease, 20k interval.

description: Cam chain tensioner (Twin Cam hydraulic) inspection
notes: Twin Cam pre-2007 shoes wear fast; late-model hydraulic units are better but still inspect at 20k.
Honda CBR service manual — shim-under-bucket. 16k interval for CBR600RR / CBR1000RR / CBR929. Forum tip: most stay in spec past 30k.
Gold Wing service manual — 10W-30. Touring owners stretch to 8k; urban riders should stay closer to 4k.
Shadow OEM paper filter — not washable. Replace at 12k; dusty climates earlier.
Honda service spec — Pro Honda HP coolant. 24-month interval independent of mileage.
Only applies to pre-FI CB models. Forum tip: a mercury stick is the most reliable tool; vacuum gauges drift.
Hypoid 80W-90 per Honda spec. Gold Wing shaft lasts forever if fluid is kept fresh.
CRF450R/CRF450L race engines — shorter interval due to high-compression single-cylinder. Forum tip: check after first 500 mi too.
Yamaha R1 service manual — 42,800 km. Crossplane engines rarely need shim changes before 40k.
Yamaha spec — 10W-40. MT-09 triple runs hot in traffic; 4k keeps the film intact.
R6 shim-under-bucket; valve-check labor is ~3 hours. Forum: many riders go 40k+ without adjustment.
FZ-series triple/four TBS requires a Carbtune or equivalent. Restored smoothness is dramatic when out of sync.
V Star 650/1100 — hypoid 80W-90. Factory fill is meant to last; 16k is a good rhythm check.
Generic Yamaha air-filter cadence. OEM paper or aftermarket K&N both OK.

description: Balancer doohickey inspection + torque-limiter bolt check
notes: KLR650 forum consensus — critical failure mode. Eagle Mike's billet doohickey is the universal aftermarket fix; stock unit eats itself eventually.
Kawasaki Ninja 300/400/650/ZX service manual — shim-under-bucket. Forum: Ninja 650 most commonly needs intake shim adjust at 30k.

description: Shaft drive / final gear oil change
notes: Vulcan shaft models — 80W-90 GL-5. Owners often skip; don't.
ZX-10R / ZX-6R have known CCT failure patterns — forum: APE manual tensioner is the preferred swap.
Kawasaki spec DOT-4. Fluid absorbs water; replace every 2 years regardless of mileage.
Suzuki GSX-R service manual. Forum tip: GSX-R 1000 K5-K8 most needs attention; later generations stable.
SV650/SV1000 V-twin runs hot; 3,500 mi is more realistic than the factory 6k for spirited riders.
V-Strom 650/1000 adventure riders frequently ride dusty roads — pull at half-interval to check.
Hayabusa hydraulic CCT is reliable but worth inspecting at 20k on high-mileage bikes.
KTM 1290 Super Duke / Super Adventure — OEM long-life coolant, 24-month replacement.
KTM 690 Duke/Enduro — LC4 single. Forum: shim-over-bucket, labor-heavy; plan a full day in the garage.
KTM spec — Motorex 4T 10W-50. High-RPM singles and twins need fresh oil frequently.
Ducati desmo — shorter interval than poppet engines. Air-cooled 2-valve: 7.5k. Testastretta 11°: 15k. Forum: always cross-check model year.
Ducati belt-driven engines — 5-year replacement interval regardless of miles. Skipping kills valves.
R1200GS service book — 6k interval is tight vs Japanese norms; BMW labor is pricey so plan ahead.
BMW FD — 75W-90 hypoid. FD failures can be $1000+; keep fluid fresh.
Universal DOT-4 2-year rule — fluid absorbs moisture, drops boiling point, and darkens. Applies to every motorcycle.
Universal chain maintenance — every 500-1000 mi depending on conditions. Skip for belt/shaft-drive bikes.
Universal — monthly pressure check. Low pressure is the #1 cause of premature tire wear and poor handling.
Universal iridium plugs. Copper plugs halve this to 7.5k. Inspect gap even when not replacing.
Universal fallback for bikes with no make-specific template. Shorten in dusty climates.
